channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Barcelona advances to Champions League semifinals despite first loss of the year
item.description: DORTMUND, Germany (AP) — Serhou Guirassy and Borussia Dortmund did what no else has been able to in 2025 — they beat Barcelona. It still wasn't enough to reach the Champions League semifinals.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Butler, Curry lead Warriors past Grizzlies 121-116 to secure seventh seed in West playoffs
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jimmy Butler and Stephen Curry threw their arms around each other and embraced. Playoff Jimmy in all his brilliance on the big stage took all the pressure off Curry, and now they're taking the Golden State Warriors to the playoffs.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: An early look at the Warriors’ first-round matchup with the Rockets
item.description: The Warriors and Rockets will add another playoff series to their ledger, but Houston's roster has a new look since the last matchup.
item.dc:creator: Evan Webeck, The Mercury News

item.title: Anthony, Magic claim 7th seed with 120-95 win over Hawks in Play-In Tournament
item.description: Cole Anthony came off the Magic bench with 26 points and six assists to lead the Orlando Magic to a 120-95 win over the Atlanta Hawks on Tuesday night in the first game of the Play-In Tournament.
item.dc:creator: Dick Scanlon, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Sharks captain Logan Couture ends his hockey career because of debilitating injury
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Logan Couture began dreaming of playing in the NHL as a 3-year-old carrying around a stick nearly everywhere he went.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Professional soccer could be headed to the North Bay
item.description: (KRON) -- The next professional sports team in the Bay Area could be bound for the North Bay. The City of Santa Rosa announced it has entered into exclusive negotiations with the United Soccer League (USL) to explore the possibilities of bringing both a men's team and a women's team to the city.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Fashion, heritage and talent: Players turn the WNBA draft into a cultural showcase
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The WNBA draft has increasingly become a platform for more than just athleticism — it is a celebration of identity, heritage, culture, and self-expression. Paige Bueckers may have been the No. 1 pick, but it was Kiki Iriafen who arguably stole the show when it came to fashion.
item.dc:creator: BRYANNA MAXWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Objectors have final say and $2.8 billion NCAA lawsuit settlement goes to judge for a decision
item.description: The $2.8 billion settlement that will reshape college athletics is now before a federal judge for a final decision.
item.dc:creator: Eddie Pells, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins Masters playoff to complete the career Grand Slam
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga. (AP) — The closer Rory McIlroy came to fulfilling his lifetime dream — winning the Masters — the more it kept slipping away. Sunday at Augusta National felt like his last 11 years in the majors, blunders mixed in with sheer brilliance. A two-shot lead gone in two holes.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Connor stars as Jets clinch Central Division title with shootout victory over Blackhawks
item.description: Kyle Connor and Cole Perfetti scored in the shootout, and the Winnipeg Jets clinched the franchise’s first Central Division title and the No. 1 seed for the Western Conference playoffs with a wild 5-4 victory over the Chicago Blackhawks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fan reaches into Mike Trout's glove to snatch a catch from Angels outfielder
item.description: A fan grabbed the ball out of Mike Trout's glove after the Los Angeles Angels star reached into the right field stands Saturday night to make what appeared to be a great catch against the Houston Astros
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Race fans brace the rain for 1st race of Spring Tripleheader in Bristol Motor Speedway
item.description: Despite the weather, fans still showed up to enjoy the Bristol Motor Speedway on Friday night to see their favorite NASCAR drivers.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: White Sox face the Red Sox with 1-0 series lead
item.description: Boston Red Sox (7-8, third in the AL East) vs. Chicago White Sox (3-10, fifth in the AL Central) Chicago; Saturday, 4:10 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Chicago Fire face Inter Miami in Eastern Conference action
item.description: Inter Miami CF (4-0-2, second in the Eastern Conference) vs. Chicago Fire (3-2-2, sixth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Sunday, 4:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fruita and Palisade show off defensive skill in 2-1 Wildcats win
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — We had an absolute showdown between two worthy Southwestern League foes at Suplizio Field Friday. Fruita pitchers Gavin Cardoza (1.62 ERA Friday) and Noah Brackett (0 ERA Friday) shut down Palisade's offense after a first-inning run.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: BYU lands point guard Robert Wright III from Baylor
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - It didn't take long for Kevin Young to find his new point guard. According to multiple reports, Baylor's Robert Wright III is transferring to BYU for his sophomore season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
